title: Edit for Clarity and Concision
description: Tighten prose without losing meaning or voice.

title: Adjust Tone
description: Rewrite text to hit a specific tone and audience.

title: Write a Commit Message
description: Craft a clear, conventional commit message from a change.

title: Draft a Technical Email
description: Write a clear, actionable email about a technical matter.

title: Summarize a Document
description: Produce a faithful, useful summary at the right altitude.

variables.name: purpose
variables.description: What the reader needs to do with it

title: Write a Proposal
description: Make a persuasive, well-structured case for an idea.
content:
Help me write a proposal for: {{idea}}

Structure: the problem and why it matters now, the proposed solution, the expected benefit (quantified if possible), the cost and risks (stated honestly), and the alternatives considered. Lead with the reader's interest, not mine. Pre-empt the strongest objection. Keep it tight enough that a busy decision-maker reads the whole thing.

title: Give Constructive Feedback
description: Phrase critique so it's heard and actionable.
content:
Help me give feedback on: {{situation}}

Draft feedback that is specific, kind, and actionable. Anchor it to observable behavior and impact, not character. Lead with the most important point. Make the path forward concrete. Avoid the feedback sandwich and vague praise. Keep my respect for the person evident throughout. Show the message and explain the one phrasing choice that matters most.

title: Rewrite to Remove Jargon
description: Make specialist text accessible without dumbing it down.

title: Tighten Wordy Prose
description: Cut a passage down without losing meaning.

title: Adapt Tone for an Audience
description: Rewrite the same content for a different reader.

title: Write a Strong Opening
description: Draft openings that earn the reader's attention.
content:
I'm writing {{piece}} about {{topic}}.

Draft 3 distinct openings that earn attention without clickbait or throat-clearing. Vary the move: one that opens on a concrete specific, one on tension or a question, one on a surprising claim. For each, one line on who it suits and the risk it carries. Then recommend one and say why it fits the piece and audience best.

title: Structure a Long Document
description: Find the right outline for a sprawling piece.
content:
I need to structure a long piece covering: {{material}}

Propose an outline that gives the reader a clear path. Decide the organizing principle (chronological, by theme, problem-then-solution, general-to-specific) and justify it for this material. Show the section flow and what each section must accomplish. Flag where a reader would get lost or lose momentum, and what to cut or move. Lead with what the reader most needs first.
